# Rural Healthcare Access Gap

Rural communities face a critical healthcare access deficit. Over 60 million Americans live in areas classified as Health Professional Shortage Areas (HPSAs), where the ratio of population to primary care physicians exceeds 3,500:1. Patients in these regions travel an average of 45 miles for specialist consultations, leading to delayed diagnoses and poorer health outcomes.

The problem is compounded by provider economics. Rural clinics operate on thin margins (average 2-4% net income) and cannot justify hiring full-time specialists. Existing telemedicine platforms focus on direct-to-consumer models, leaving the B2B provider-to-specialist connection underserved. HealthBridge targets this gap: a marketplace where rural providers can book specialist consultations for their patients, maintaining the provider relationship while expanding access to care.
